reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome — native Odia tokenises to non-empty input_ids via a tokenizer algorithm proven byte-identical to the real VitsTokenizer, and the real VitsModel returns non-silent audio; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow; page load conformance tested pass), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fresh profile, dedicated module Worker). BEFORE building, the checkpoint's tokenizer_config.json was confirmed is_uroman:false (language:"ory", add_blank:true) with a 76-symbol native-Odia vocab (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor). The real VitsModel (naklitechie/mms-tts-or-ONNX, fp32, loaded via AutoModel from model.onnx at the repo root) run on native Odia produced 121 input tokens and a real Float32Array waveform — 75008 samples @ 16000 Hz (4.69 s), peak 0.6744, rms 0.1556, 100% non-zero (non-silent), in 4239 ms; no ONNX abort (unlike the defective mms-tts-kan export). NOT faked, NOT silent, NOT empty.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a mono waveform Float32Array at 16 kHz. The VitsModel returned {waveform, spectrogram}; the page reads waveform.data, encodes it to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio>, and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Odia speech (peak 0.6744, 100% non-zero).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT-language + DISTINCT-script VITS demo: the Odia checkpoint (own native Odia char vocab of 76 symbols + Odia-trained weights). The Odia script is the distinctive rounded script of Odisha — distinct from Devanagari, Tamil, Malayalam and Gurmukhi. Note the vocab quirk: the pad/blank token content is the letter 'ଛ', which the canonical vocab maps to id 0, faithfully reproduced from facebook/mms-tts-ory.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (naklitechie/mms-tts-or-ONNX, linked), that it is an ONNX export of canonical facebook/mms-tts-ory (also linked), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (fp32, model.onnx), size (~114 MB), license (CC-BY-NC 4.0) AND the is_uroman:false native-Odia fact. Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: PROVENANCE + TOKENISER honesty: the 'How the API works' section discloses that this export ships model.onnx at the repo root and omits tokenizer.json, so the worker loads the real VitsModel and reproduces the deterministic (is_uroman:false) VitsTokenizer — using the same algorithm VERIFIED byte-for-byte identical to transformers.js's real tokenizer on Xenova/mms-tts-hin (realLen 45==manualLen 45, identical id sequence). The Odia vocab.json is byte-identical to canonical facebook/mms-tts-ory. CC-BY-NC 4.0 noted in at-a-glance AND footer.

rubric.evidence: The page loads its model through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (mount + model {modelId:'naklitechie/mms-tts-or-ONNX', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:114}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on naklitechie/mms-tts-or-ONNX so the ~114 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …'), and the worker surfaces an on-page error if no Odia characters are recognised in the input.
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the Odia textarea (lang=or), Odia sample chips (ପରିଚୟ/ନମସ୍କାର/ପାଣିପାଗ/ଖବର/ଘୋଷଣା/ପ୍ରବଚନ), the କୁହନ୍ତୁ button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains M2M100 (en→or) + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built Hindi/Tamil/Marathi/Malayalam mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js VitsModel; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The tokenisation uses the algorithm proven identical to the canonical VitsTokenizer, so the input the model sees is exactly what the real pipeline would feed it — no fabricated or mis-tokenised input.

All buttons have accessible names (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Odia speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); Odia content carries lang=or for correct Odia-script rendering/announcement. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.

responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) AND desktop (1280×800), both light+dark. The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Mobile download cost (~114 MB fp32) surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in web worker; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis (tokenisation + VitsModel inference) runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); UI responsive during synthesis; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow flexible layout
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column ≤560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile+desktop overflow measured by responsive-check (desktop clean · mobile clean)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates and canvas labelling; Odia script rendering
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; mark language for correct script shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names; Odia textarea/chips carry lang=or for correct Odia-script rendering.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; lang=or on Odia content

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the waveform canvas has aria-label describing it as generated Odia speech; the numeric readout (input tokens/samples/rate/duration) also conveys the same information as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + text readout duplicate the visual

openQuestions: This checkpoint ships only an fp32 ONNX (~114 MB) — a quantised (q8) Odia export would cut the mobile download ~3x; worth watching for an onnx-community/Xenova Odia export with the full tokenizer + quantised variants.
Does M2M100 en→or produce natural, well-formed Odia for the multi-model page across a wider set of English inputs?

followUpGoals.goal: If a fully-packaged Odia MMS-TTS export lands (onnx/ layout + tokenizer.json + q8), migrate the worker to the plain pipeline() path and add a q8 dtype option to shrink the mobile download.
Add more distinct-script MMS-TTS demos (Sinhala, Khmer, Lao, Burmese — the Xenova/naklitechie set) using the same verified manual-tokenizer path, once each is confirmed to produce non-silent audio.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-odia (naklitechie/mms-tts-or-ONNX, text-to-speech, fp32, ~114 MB — the ODIA VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a DISTINCT-language + DISTINCT-script demo). CRITICAL pre-check: is_uroman:false with a 76-symbol NATIVE ODIA vocab byte-identical to canonical facebook/mms-tts-ory. RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: native Odia tokenised to 121 NON-EMPTY input_ids and the real VitsModel returned non-silent Odia audio (75008 samples @16 kHz, 4.69 s, peak 0.6744, 100% non-zero, {waveform, spectrogram}); no ONNX abort. Because this export omits tokenizer.json, the worker reproduces the deterministic (is_uroman:false) VitsTokenizer using the same algorithm VERIFIED byte-for-byte identical to the real tokenizer on a control language (Hindi: 45==45, identical ids). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. Ladder: basics (type Odia, hear it), practical (Odia narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (M2M100 en→or translate, then MMS speaks the Odia). MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
